evidence_quotes.quote: "pull the plug" on video games that are always online and requires an internet connection.

paragraphs.text: Greetings! I just want chip is as someone who has been using video games as a hobby since late 90s. I've played many games throughout my life many whom I can return to, but sadly many I can't. The reason is that in todays world, video game companies can whenever they want, "pull the plug" on video games that are always online and requires an internet connection. We consumers are currently facing a time where even if we pay for something, video game companies can remove our ability to play for what we've paid for. But to me this isn't even the worst part. When it comes to video games, videos games are by definition art. We are not talking about Mona Lisa painting, but we are talking about music that might even have a full on orchestra. I used to play a game called WildStar, and I urge you to youtube "Wildstar theme" if you want a sample. On top of this we have gorgeous textures, lighting effects, voice acting, stories made by authors and much much more. These things individually might not mean much, but combine all of it and you can have one of the best experiences in your life if you find the specific video game for you! If this isn't art, then I don't know what art is but to me it is! And I merely want to preserve art. I will sadly never be able to play Wildstar again as the video game company who owned it stopped providing the possibility to play online and never bothered with a offline mode, but I hope that we can prevent future scenarios like this to occur again. Our world is more digital than ever and it is time we understand the following: If I as a consumer have paid for something, whether it is physical or something digital (ex video game). No one should be able to take it away from me and if someone does, wouldn't that be theft?
